info.title: Rekko AI — Prediction Market Intelligence
info.description: The brain for prediction market trading. Deep AI research, Bayesian causal decomposition, portfolio-aware strategy signals, cross-platform arbitrage, and execution guidance — all in one API. Covers Kalshi, Polymarket, and Robinhood. Clean flat JSON designed for developers and autonomous trading agents.

get.description: Health check with data freshness and platform coverage stats.
Return tier pricing for all feed endpoints (no auth required).

Internal metrics for tracking acquisition-readiness KPIs.

Returns active developers, monthly API calls, calls by tier/auth method,
and platform coverage. Admin access only.
Signal accuracy and calibration metrics (free, no auth required).
List current prediction markets with pricing data.
Get a single market listing by platform and ID.
Get historical price and volume data for backtesting.
Optimal trade execution analysis from bid/ask spread patterns.
Resolution timing, mechanism, and time-value analysis.

get.summary: Get latest analysis for a market
get.description: Get the latest AI analysis for a market, keyed by platform and market ID.
List recent analysis summaries, keyed by market.

post.description: Start an async analysis for a specific market. Poll the status URL for completion.

get.description: Check the status of an async analysis.

post.description: Batch screen markets with lightweight scoring (no LLM pipeline).

Generate an actionable strategy signal for a market.

Default: returns 202 with analysis_id for polling.
With ?wait=true: blocks until pipeline completes (30-90 seconds).
If a cached analysis exists (<24h): returns immediately.
Use "force": true to bypass cache.

parameters.name: wait

schema.description: Block until analysis completes (up to 5 min)

parameters.description: Block until analysis completes (up to 5 min)

post.description:
Analyze how a hypothetical scenario would affect a market's probability.

Strategy tier ($2.00/call).

risks.title: Risks
risks.description: Ways the analysis could be wrong

analysis_type.title: Analysis Type
analysis_type.description: Whether this was a full 4-stage or light 2-call analysis

Analysis.title: Analysis
Analysis.description:
AI-generated research analysis for a prediction market.

Core fields are always present. Detailed breakdowns available
via ?expand=scenarios,causal,meta.

key_factors: PCE inflation at 2.1% — within Fed target range
Three FOMC members signaled openness to cuts in recent speeches
Jobless claims trending upward (225K → 238K over 4 weeks)
risks: Strong March employment report could reverse dovish signals
Tariff escalation could reignite inflation expectations
example.summary: Fed futures imply 68% probability of a March cut. PCE inflation at 2.1% is within the Fed's comfort zone, and three FOMC members have signaled openness to easing. Our research across 14 sources estimates 71% true probability — a 9-point edge over the current market price of 62c.
example.title: Will the Fed cut rates at the March 2026 meeting?

what_would_change_mind.title: What Would Change Mind
what_would_change_mind.description: Events that would change the recommendation

ensemble_disagreement.title: Ensemble Disagreement
ensemble_disagreement.description: Std dev across ensemble models (0 = full agreement)

calibration_applied.title: Calibration Applied
calibration_applied.description: Whether post-hoc Platt scaling was applied

AnalysisMeta.title: AnalysisMeta
AnalysisMeta.description: Internal metadata about how an analysis was produced (opt-in via ?expand=meta).

example.edge_assessment: Market at 62c implies 62% probability. Our 71% estimate gives +9c edge — significant at this confidence level.

example.description: Fed delivers 25bp cut citing cooling inflation and softening labor market
example.name: Bull (rate cut)
example.trigger: PCE stays below 2.2%, jobless claims rise above 230K

CausalDecomposition.title: CausalDecomposition
CausalDecomposition.description:
Weighted causal decomposition of a market probability estimate.

Breaks a probability estimate into evidence-backed factors with
Bayesian prior/posterior updates.

factors.claim: Inflation is within Fed's comfort zone

claim.title: Claim
claim.description: The factor statement, e.g. 'Fed will hold rates'

direction.title: Direction
direction.description: Whether this factor pushes toward YES or NO resolution

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: Relative importance weight (top-level factors sum to ~1.0)

CausalFactor.title: CausalFactor
CausalFactor.description: A single factor in a causal decomposition.

example.claim: Inflation is within Fed's comfort zone
evidence: Core CPI declining for 3 consecutive months

consensus_probability.title: Consensus Probability
consensus_probability.description: Volume-weighted implied probability from agent trades

ConsensusView.title: ConsensusView
ConsensusView.description: Aggregated consensus from agent trading activity.

concentration_warnings: KXFED-26MAR19 and KXCPI-26MAR are highly correlated (0.73) — holding both creates concentration risk on Fed policy

spread_percentile.title: Spread Percentile
spread_percentile.description: Current spread percentile vs 48h history (lower = tighter than usual)

liquidity_score.title: Liquidity Score
liquidity_score.description: Liquidity quality 0-100 based on spread + volume

estimated_slippage_pct.title: Estimated Slippage Pct
estimated_slippage_pct.description: Estimated slippage as percentage of price

suggested_limit_price.title: Suggested Limit Price

upcoming_event.title: Upcoming Event

hours_to_event.title: Hours To Event
hours_to_event.description: Hours until the next relevant event

ExecutionGuidance.title: ExecutionGuidance
ExecutionGuidance.description: Optimal trade execution analysis from tick data.

example.rationale: Spread is wider than usual (72nd percentile). Use a limit order at 0.61 to capture midpoint. Avoid market orders — slippage ~0.8% at current depth.
example.title: Will the Fed cut rates at the March 2026 meeting?

example.rationale: Negatively correlated with your Fed rate cut position — if the economy stays strong (no cut), unemployment stays low (NO wins)
